revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a crisp SNES-era top-down Japanese satoyama fantasy RPG pixel-art field prop asset, 128 px wide by 384 px tall, one continuous vertical composition, no grid, no borders, no text. Fill all empty/background space with solid #FF00FF magenta chroma key only, no gradients. Crisp low-resolution pixels, no antialiasing blur, consistent bright natural palette, one-pixel dark outline where useful, top-left golden afternoon light. Original fantasy game asset only; no recognizable existing game characters, logos, emblems, UI, modern objects, weapons, armor, animals, or companion creatures.

Subject: rice terrace spillway module integrated into three vertical zones, each roughly 128 px tall, aligned as one continuous prop.

Top zone: shallow blue-green rice paddy water surface with young rice seedlings. Near the bottom of this zone, the water calmly gathers and narrows toward a small stone-and-mud drainage opening. Subtle ripple highlights only, no dramatic waterfall. Mossy field bank edges fade into the left and right frame edges so it can connect naturally to adjacent mossy stone wall tiles.

Middle zone: front-facing mossy stone retaining wall made of stacked ishigaki stones, with one narrow vertical water channel cut through the exact center, about 24 px wide. Left and right stone sections are continuous wall mass, not separate rock pillars. Stones have moss, bright upper rim highlights, and dark lower contact shadows. The center channel sends water straight down with white-blue streak highlights and dark wet stone on both sides. Cut edges are slightly worn and mossy.

Bottom zone: small circular splash pool receiving the falling water from above. White foam at the center impact, clear blue-green ripples expanding outward, a few small wet mossy stones around the edges, reed tufts at the sides, subtle outflow channel exiting toward lower right, and dark wet shadow under the pool edge. The upper edge connects directly to the falling water from the middle zone.

Composition rules: the full spillway prop stays inside the 128x384 canvas with clear solid #FF00FF margin wherever there is no prop. No detached fragments, no isolated pixel dust, no leftover residue, no cell separator lines, no frame numbers, no labels, no watermark. Top-down/front-facing hybrid appropriate for a 2D RPG tile prop; no isometric perspective, no vanishing point.
